# MiniCRUFT World Core Phase 2

GOAL: Implement the smallest useful world-core slice in `MiniCRUFT.World` that establishes deterministic chunk data, subchunk storage, coordinate helpers, and chunk retrieval from `WorldHost`.
TIMELINE: current session

## Task Checklist

### Phase 1: Model boundaries
- [*] Confirm exact minimal world-core file set
- [ ] Confirm safe interfaces for `Game -> World`

### Phase 2: World data model
- [ ] Add `BlockId` baseline
- [ ] Add chunk coordinate and local block coordinate helpers
- [ ] Add dense `Subchunk` storage
- [ ] Add `Chunk` with 8 subchunks
- [ ] Add `ChunkState` enum

### Phase 3: World behavior
- [ ] Add deterministic simple generator
- [ ] Add `WorldHost.GetOrCreateChunk(x, z)`
- [ ] Add basic world counters/debug-friendly state

### Phase 4: Validation
- [ ] Validate same seed => same chunk contents
- [ ] Validate chunk dimensions/constants usage stays correct
- [ ] Update next-step backlog

## Decisions
- Keep the slice inside `MiniCRUFT.World` only unless a tiny `Game` touch is required
- Prefer flat/simple deterministic terrain first; avoid caves/features for now
- Dense storage first, no palette compression yet
- Preserve `16x16x128` chunk and `16x16x16` subchunk boundaries

## Owners
- Cody: implementation
- Archy: boundary review

## Success Criteria
- [ ] World data model exists and compiles
- [ ] `WorldHost` can return deterministic chunk instances by coordinate
- [ ] No renderer/persistence coupling is introduced
- [ ] The result is a clean base for later meshing/streaming/save-load work
